name: iPaaS Wave 2: Data Layer
outcome_goal: Build data mapping engine and schema registry for normalizing external API data

jobs.label: ipaas_w2_data_mapper
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Build a lightweight data mapping / transformation engine for normalizing API responses across services.

CONTEXT:
- Praxis agents call external APIs via connectors and webhooks, but each returns data in different shapes.
- There's no shared way to extract, rename, coerce, or flatten fields from API responses.
- The structured_output.py adapter parses LLM output but doesn't handle API data transformation.
- We need a simple, deterministic transform engine — not a full ETL framework.

DELIVERABLES:

1. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/data_mapper.py:

   TransformSpec dataclass:
     - mappings: list[FieldMapping]  — ordered field extraction rules
     - defaults: dict  — default values for missing fields
     - drop_unmapped: bool = False  — if True, output only mapped fields

   FieldMapping dataclass:
     - source: str  — dot-notation path (e.g. 'data.customer.email', 'items[0].id')
     - target: str  — output field name (e.g. 'customer_email')
     - coerce: Optional[str]  — type coercion: 'str', 'int', 'float', 'bool', 'iso_date'
     - fallback: Optional[Any]  — value if source path doesn't exist

   Functions:
   - extract_path(data: dict, path: str) -> Any
     - Supports dot notation: 'a.b.c'
     - Supports array indexing: 'items[0]', 'items[-1]'
     - Supports wildcard: 'items[*].name' -> list of all names
     - Returns None if path doesn't exist (no exceptions)

   - coerce_value(value: Any, target_type: str) -> Any
     - 'str' -> str(value)
     - 'int' -> int(value) with float truncation
     - 'float' -> float(value)
     - 'bool' -> handles 'true'/'false'/0/1/None
     - 'iso_date' -> parse common date formats to ISO 8601 string
     - Returns value unchanged if coercion fails (log warning, don't crash)

   - apply_transform(data: dict, spec: TransformSpec) -> dict
     - Applies each mapping in order
     - Extracts source path, coerces if specified, writes to target
     - Applies defaults for missing targets
     - Drops unmapped fields if drop_unmapped=True
     - Returns flat output dict

   - transform_from_dict(spec_dict: dict) -> TransformSpec
     - Parses a JSON/dict representation into TransformSpec
     - Format: {"mappings": [{"source": "...", "target": "...", "coerce": "..."}], "defaults": {}, "drop_unmapped": false}

2. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/test_data_mapper.py:
   - Test dot-notation extraction (nested objects)
   - Test array indexing ([0], [-1], [*])
   - Test wildcard extraction returns list
   - Test type coercion (each type)
   - Test missing path returns fallback
   - Test drop_unmapped=True filters output
   - Test transform_from_dict round-trip
   - Test real-world example: Stripe payment event -> normalized {amount_cents, currency, customer_email, status}
   - Test real-world example: GitHub push event -> normalized {repo, branch, commit_sha, author}

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- Pure functions, no side effects, no database access
- Dataclasses for all types
- No external dependencies (stdlib only)
- Style similar to Code&DBs/Workflow/adapters/structured_output.py

DO NOT:
- Implement a full query language (no JSONata parser, no jq grammar)
- Add template string interpolation
- Create more than 2 files

jobs.label: ipaas_w2_schema_registry
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Build a schema registry that imports OpenAPI specs and stores versioned API schemas in Postgres.

CONTEXT:
- Praxis connectors are generated from research, not from specs. There's no shared vocabulary for what external APIs expose.
- The connector builder (Skills/dag-connector/SKILL.md) generates Pydantic models per-connector but they're not registered.
- Agents need to know what fields/endpoints a service exposes without reading connector source code.
- We need: import an OpenAPI spec -> store endpoints/schemas -> query by service/resource.

DELIVERABLES:

1. Create Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/094_schema_registry.sql:

   Table: api_schemas (
     schema_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT 'asch_' || substr(md5(random()::text), 1, 12),
     provider_slug TEXT NOT NULL,  -- e.g. 'stripe', 'github', 'slack'
     version TEXT NOT NULL,  -- semver or date-based: '2024-01-01', '3.1.0'
     title TEXT,
     description TEXT,
     base_url TEXT,
     auth_type TEXT,  -- 'bearer', 'api_key', 'oauth2'
     raw_spec JSONB,  -- original OpenAPI JSON (compressed reference)
     created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
     UNIQUE(provider_slug, version)
   )

   Table: api_endpoints (
     endpoint_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT 'aend_' || substr(md5(random()::text), 1, 12),
     schema_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES api_schemas(schema_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     path TEXT NOT NULL,  -- '/v1/customers/{id}'
     method TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'GET', 'POST', etc.
     operation_id TEXT,  -- 'getCustomer'
     summary TEXT,
     request_schema JSONB,  -- simplified field spec
     response_schema JSONB,  -- simplified field spec
     UNIQUE(schema_id, path, method)
   )

   Table: api_models (
     model_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT 'amod_' || substr(md5(random()::text), 1, 12),
     schema_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES api_schemas(schema_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     name TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'Customer', 'PaymentIntent'
     fields JSONB NOT NULL,  -- [{"name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true}, ...]
     UNIQUE(schema_id, name)
   )

   Indexes:
   - idx_api_schemas_provider ON api_schemas(provider_slug)
   - idx_api_endpoints_schema ON api_endpoints(schema_id)
   - idx_api_models_schema ON api_models(schema_id)

2. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/schema_registry.py:

   - import_openapi_spec(conn, spec_json: dict, provider_slug: str, version: str) -> str (schema_id)
     - Parses OpenAPI 3.x spec JSON
     - Extracts info.title, info.description, servers[0].url
     - Extracts paths -> api_endpoints rows (path, method, operationId, summary)
     - Extracts components.schemas -> api_models rows (name, fields)
     - Simplifies JSON Schema types to flat field lists: [{name, type, required, description}]
     - Stores raw_spec as reference
     - Returns schema_id

   - get_schema(conn, provider_slug: str, version: Optional[str] = None) -> dict
     - Returns latest version if version is None
     - Returns {schema_id, provider_slug, version, title, base_url, endpoints: [...], models: [...]}

   - list_schemas(conn) -> list[dict]
     - Returns [{provider_slug, version, title, endpoint_count, model_count}]

   - search_endpoints(conn, provider_slug: str, query: str) -> list[dict]
     - Searches endpoint path, operation_id, summary by ILIKE
     - Returns matching endpoints with their request/response schemas

   - get_model(conn, provider_slug: str, model_name: str) -> Optional[dict]
     - Returns model fields for a given provider + model name

3. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/test_schema_registry.py:
   - Test import of a minimal OpenAPI 3.0 spec (inline fixture, not a real API spec)
   - Test endpoint extraction (path, method, operationId)
   - Test model extraction (name, fields with types)
   - Test get_schema returns latest version
   - Test search_endpoints by keyword
   - Test version uniqueness constraint

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- SQL queries use parameterized format from storage.postgres (conn.execute(sql, *params))
- Migration follows existing numbering (094)
- Functions take conn as first arg (same as event_log.py pattern)

DO NOT:
- Parse YAML (only JSON input — callers convert YAML to JSON first)
- Handle OpenAPI 2.x (Swagger) format
- Validate the spec against the OpenAPI meta-schema
- Add $ref resolution for deeply nested schemas (flatten to top-level only)

jobs.label: ipaas_w2_review
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Review Wave 2 iPaaS deliverables for correctness and Praxis pattern adherence.

REVIEW CHECKLIST:

1. data_mapper.py:
   - Path extraction handles edge cases (empty dict, None, missing keys)
   - Array indexing bounds-safe (negative index, out of range)
   - Wildcard [*] returns list, not single item
   - Coercion is safe (no exceptions on bad input)
   - TransformSpec is JSON-serializable

2. schema_registry.py:
   - OpenAPI 3.x parsing covers common spec shapes
   - JSON Schema simplification doesn't lose critical type info
   - SQL queries are parameterized (no injection)
   - get_schema returns latest version correctly (ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1)
   - Search is case-insensitive

3. Migration 094:
   - No conflicts with existing tables
   - CASCADE delete is appropriate (deleting a schema removes its endpoints/models)
   - Unique constraints prevent duplicate imports

4. Tests:
   - Fixtures are self-contained (no external API calls)
   - Cover happy path and edge cases
   - Runnable with standard pytest command

Output structured PASS/FAIL per item with required fixes.
